brief_document.title: Workflow status, filters, and summaries
brief_document.purpose: Define the derived, queryable view of a workflow: its status (a projection over event history) and the filter and summary types the query surface uses.
brief_document.task: Define WorkflowStatus as an enum and a projection function that computes it from an event history. Define WorkflowFilter (the query input) and WorkflowSummary (the lightweight query output). Status must never be a free-standing mutable field — it is always derived from events.

requirements.title: WorkflowStatus and projection
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define WorkflowStatus with variants Running, Completed, Failed, Cancelled, TimedOut. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a function that computes WorkflowStatus from a slice of events by inspecting the terminal lifecycle event, defaulting to Running when none is present.
acceptance: status.rs defines WorkflowStatus { Running, Completed, Failed, Cancelled, TimedOut } deriving Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq.
A function status_from_events(&[Event]) -> WorkflowStatus returns Running when no terminal event exists, and the matching terminal status otherwise.

WorkflowCompleted maps to Completed, WorkflowFailed to Failed, WorkflowCancelled to Cancelled, and WorkflowTimedOut to TimedOut — every terminal WorkflowStatus variant has exactly one corresponding terminal event (AC-002 R2), so the projection is total.

WorkflowStatus does not derive or implement Default and exposes no new()/constructor; status_from_events is the only function in aion-core that produces a WorkflowStatus from history, and a doc comment states that status must be obtained only by projection from event history, never assigned directly (enforces C18 / CO7).

Unit tests cover: empty history (Running), each terminal event including WorkflowTimedOut, and a history with events after start but before any terminal event (Running); a test asserts the projection is deterministic (the same history always yields the same status).

requirements.title: WorkflowFilter
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define WorkflowFilter supporting optional filtering by workflow type, status, time range, and parent workflow. An empty filter SHALL match all workflows.
acceptance: filter.rs defines WorkflowFilter with optional fields: workflow_type, status, started_after, started_before, parent.
WorkflowFilter has a builder or a Default that yields a match-all filter.
A matches(&self, &WorkflowSummary) -> bool helper evaluates a summary against the filter (used by the in-memory store and as reference semantics for durable backends).
Unit tests cover match-all, single-field filters, and a combined filter.

requirements.title: WorkflowSummary
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define WorkflowSummary as the lightweight projection of a workflow: id, type, status, start time, and optional end time.
acceptance: WorkflowSummary carries workflow_id: WorkflowId, workflow_type: String, status: WorkflowStatus, started_at: DateTime<Utc>, ended_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>.
WorkflowSummary derives Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug, PartialEq.
A constructor or helper builds a summary from a workflow's event history (start event plus projected status plus terminal timestamp).
A unit test builds a summary from a small history and asserts the fields.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define the EventStore trait or query method — those are AC-005; this brief defines the types the query method uses.
SHALL NOT cache status anywhere; status is always computed from events here.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: WorkflowStatus enum has variants Running, Completed, Failed, Cancelled, TimedOut.
WorkflowStatus is computed by projecting over event history (the last lifecycle event determines the status).
No code path sets WorkflowStatus independently of the events that justify it.
WorkflowFilter supports filtering by workflow type, status, time range, and parent workflow.
WorkflowSummary carries workflow id, type, status, start time, and end time.

stories.text: As an operator, I want to query workflows by type, status, and time range so that I can see what is running and what has failed.
As an operator, I want a lightweight summary per workflow so that listing many workflows does not require loading every full history.

constraints.text: lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion-core and aion-store are leaf crates — they depend on no other Aion crate. Structural; must hold.
Every type that crosses the wire or the store derives Serialize + Deserialize (these are data types, not credentials).
WorkflowStatus is always derivable from event history; no independent status mutation.
InMemoryStore must pass the same behavioural test suite future durable backends are held to — it is the oracle, so it must be correct.
Identifiers are newtypes; no bare Uuid or String in public signatures where an ID is meant.

resolved_context.intention: Define the vocabulary every other part of Aion speaks: the event types, identifiers, workflow status, error taxonomy, and the persistence contract. When the engine records an activity completion, a client lists running workflows, a store persists history, or a worker reports a result, they all use the types defined here. The types must make illegal states unrepresentable — a completed workflow cannot also be running, an activity result cannot exist without a preceding schedule. Nothing here depends on beamr, the engine, networking, or any storage backend. This is the still point at the centre: pure types and one trait, so the rest of Aion can be built against stable contracts.
